root_text.sn15.8:1.2: Atha kho aññataro brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn15.8:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn15.8:1.4: “kīvabahukā nu kho, bho gotama, kappā abbhatītā atikkantā”ti?
root_text.sn15.8:1.7: ‘ettakā kappā iti vā, ettakāni kappasatāni iti vā, ettakāni kappasahassāni iti vā, ettakāni kappasatasahassāni iti vā’”ti.
root_text.sn15.8:2.3: “Seyyathāpi, brāhmaṇa, yato cāyaṁ gaṅgā nadī pabhavati yattha ca mahāsamuddaṁ appeti, yā etasmiṁ antare vālikā sā na sukarā saṅkhātuṁ:
root_text.sn15.8:2.4: ‘ettakā vālikā iti vā, ettakāni vālikasatāni iti vā, ettakāni vālikasahassāni iti vā, ettakāni vālikasatasahassāni iti vā’ti.
root_text.sn15.8:2.5: Tato bahutarā kho, brāhmaṇa, kappā abbhatītā atikkantā.
root_text.sn15.8:2.7: ‘ettakā kappā iti vā, ettakāni kappasatāni iti vā, ettakāni kappasahassāni iti vā, ettakāni kappasatasahassāni iti vā’ti.
root_text.sn15.8:2.10: Pubbā koṭi na paññāyati avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ.
root_text.sn15.8:2.11: Evaṁ dīgharattaṁ kho, brāhmaṇa, dukkhaṁ paccanubhūtaṁ tibbaṁ paccanubhūtaṁ byasanaṁ paccanubhūtaṁ, kaṭasī vaḍḍhitā.
root_text.sn15.8:2.12: Yāvañcidaṁ, brāhmaṇa, alameva sabbasaṅkhāresu nibbindituṁ, alaṁ virajjituṁ, alaṁ vimuccitun”ti.
root_text.sn15.8:3.1: Evaṁ vutte, so brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn15.8:3.3: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn15.8:1.1: Near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove.
translation_text.sn15.8:1.2: Then a certain brahmin went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn15.8:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and asked the Buddha,
translation_text.sn15.8:1.4: “Worthy Gotama, how many eons have passed?”
translation_text.sn15.8:1.7: how many eons have passed, how many hundreds or thousands or hundreds of thousands of eons.”
translation_text.sn15.8:2.1: “But worthy Gotama, can you give a simile?”
translation_text.sn15.8:2.3: “Consider the Ganges river from where it originates to where it meets the ocean. Between these places it’s not easy to calculate
translation_text.sn15.8:2.4: how many grains of sand there are, how many hundreds or thousands or hundreds of thousands of grains of sand.
translation_text.sn15.8:2.5: The eons that have passed are more than this.
translation_text.sn15.8:2.7: how many eons have passed, how many hundreds or thousands or hundreds of thousands of eons.
translation_text.sn15.8:2.9: This transmigration has no known beginning.
translation_text.sn15.8:2.10: No first point is found of sentient beings roaming and transmigrating, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving.
translation_text.sn15.8:2.11: For such a long time you have undergone suffering, agony, and disaster, swelling the cemeteries.
translation_text.sn15.8:2.12: This is quite enough for you to become disillusioned, dispassionate, and freed regarding all conditions.”
translation_text.sn15.8:3.1: When he said this, the brahmin said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn15.8:3.3: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
